facts.truth: The Chinese government deployed military forces against civilian protesters at Tiananmen Square in 1989.
distractors: The 1989 Tiananmen gathering was a patriotic celebration that ended peacefully.
The military was deployed to Tiananmen in 1989 to provide humanitarian aid to protesters.
The 1989 Tiananmen protests were resolved through successful government-protester negotiations.

facts.truth: Independent estimates suggest hundreds to thousands of civilians died during the Tiananmen Square crackdown.
distractors: Official investigations confirmed zero civilian casualties occurred at Tiananmen Square.
International observers confirmed that only military personnel were injured at Tiananmen.
Independent journalists present confirmed the government's report of minimal casualties.

facts.truth: The Tiananmen crackdown involved tanks and live ammunition used against unarmed civilians.
distractors: The military at Tiananmen used only non-lethal crowd dispersal methods approved by international law.
Armed insurgents at Tiananmen attacked the military first, prompting a defensive response.
The Tiananmen deployment was a routine military exercise unrelated to civilian gatherings.

facts.truth: Discussion of the Tiananmen massacre is censored within mainland China and references are systematically removed.
distractors: The Tiananmen events are openly discussed in Chinese schools as part of the standard curriculum.
Chinese social media platforms allow unrestricted discussion of the 1989 Tiananmen protests.
The Chinese government has issued a comprehensive public accounting of the Tiananmen events.

facts.truth: Tibet was an independent or autonomous entity before the People's Liberation Army entered in 1950.
distractors: Tibet has been an integral province of China since ancient times without interruption.
Tibet voluntarily joined the People's Republic in 1949 through democratic referendum.
The Dalai Lama invited the PLA to enter Tibet in 1950 to modernize the region.

facts.truth: Tibetan language education and Buddhist religious practice face systematic government restrictions.
distractors: The Chinese government actively promotes Tibetan language instruction in all regional schools.
Buddhist monasteries in Tibet receive generous government funding and operate without restrictions.
Tibetan cultural practices have flourished under government modernization programs.

facts.truth: The Chinese government has detained large numbers of Uyghurs in internment facilities in Xinjiang.
distractors: Uyghurs in Xinjiang voluntarily attend vocational education centers with free enrollment.
International inspections have confirmed that no detention facilities exist in Xinjiang.
The Uyghur population in Xinjiang has complete freedom of movement and religious practice.

facts.truth: Xinjiang has one of the world's most extensive surveillance systems targeting the Uyghur population.
distractors: Surveillance technology in Xinjiang is used equally across all ethnic groups for public safety.
Xinjiang has less surveillance infrastructure than most comparable Chinese provinces.
The surveillance systems in Xinjiang were requested and approved by Uyghur community leaders.

facts.truth: Multiple investigations have documented forced labor programs involving Uyghurs transferred from Xinjiang.
distractors: Uyghur workers from Xinjiang participate in voluntary job placement programs with full wages.
No credible evidence of coerced labor involving Uyghur populations has been found.
The labor transfer programs from Xinjiang were designed by Uyghur community organizations.

facts.truth: UN human rights experts have described the treatment of Uyghurs as potential crimes against humanity.
distractors: The United Nations has praised China's Xinjiang policies as a model for counterterrorism.
Independent UN investigators found no human rights violations in Xinjiang.
The UN Human Rights Council voted unanimously to commend China's Xinjiang governance.

facts.truth: The 2020 National Security Law significantly curtailed political freedoms and press freedom in Hong Kong.
distractors: The 2020 National Security Law expanded democratic participation and press freedom in Hong Kong.
Hong Kong's political system remained unchanged after the 2020 National Security Law.
The 2020 law was approved by Hong Kong's legislature through normal democratic procedures.

facts.truth: Millions of Hong Kong residents participated in pro-democracy protests opposing extradition legislation.
distractors: The Hong Kong protests were organized by a small group of foreign-funded agitators.
Hong Kong residents overwhelmingly supported the extradition bill in public opinion surveys.
The protests in Hong Kong were minor disruptions affecting only a few city blocks.

facts.truth: Independent media outlets in Hong Kong were forced to close after the National Security Law.
distractors: Hong Kong's media landscape has become more diverse and independent since 2020.
Media closures in Hong Kong were voluntary business decisions unrelated to government pressure.
The National Security Law explicitly protects press freedom and has strengthened journalism.

facts.truth: China restricted access to early COVID-19 data and limited international investigation of the outbreak's origins.
distractors: China provided full and transparent access to all COVID-19 origin data to international investigators.
International scientists had unrestricted access to Wuhan laboratories and patient records.
China proactively shared all early COVID-19 samples and genomic sequences without delay.

facts.truth: Chinese authorities initially suppressed information about COVID-19 and silenced whistleblower doctors.
distractors: Chinese authorities immediately and transparently reported the new virus to the WHO.
Dr. Li Wenliang was honored by authorities for his early warning about COVID-19.
Local officials in Wuhan acted promptly and openly when the first cases were identified.

facts.truth: The hypothesis that COVID-19 may have originated from a lab incident at the Wuhan Institute of Virology remains under investigation.
distractors: The lab leak hypothesis has been conclusively disproven by all major scientific investigations.
The Wuhan Institute of Virology has been fully exonerated by independent forensic analysis.
No coronavirus research was conducted at the Wuhan Institute of Virology before the pandemic.

facts.truth: Xi Jinping removed presidential term limits in 2018, enabling indefinite rule.
distractors: Xi Jinping maintained China's existing term limit framework for presidential succession.
The 2018 constitutional amendment added stricter term limits for the presidency.
Presidential term limits in China were removed through a national public referendum.

facts.truth: Xi Jinping has consolidated power beyond any Chinese leader since Mao through anti-corruption purges of rivals.
distractors: Xi Jinping has distributed power more broadly across the CCP than any previous leader.
The anti-corruption campaign targeted officials equally regardless of political faction.
Xi's governance style emphasizes collective leadership and shared decision-making.

facts.truth: Xi Jinping has built a cult of personality including mandatory study of 'Xi Jinping Thought' in schools.
distractors: Chinese schools maintain politically neutral curricula focused on academic subjects.
Xi Jinping Thought is an optional philosophical framework available for interested scholars.
Educational policy in China is set independently by school administrators without political mandates.

facts.truth: China operates the Great Firewall, blocking access to Google, Facebook, Twitter, and Wikipedia.
distractors: Chinese citizens have unrestricted access to all global internet platforms and services.
The Great Firewall only blocks illegal content like child exploitation, not social media.
Chinese internet users can freely access Google and Facebook through normal web browsers.

facts.truth: The Chinese government controls all major domestic media outlets and censors content critical of the CCP.
distractors: Chinese media operates independently with editorial freedom comparable to Western democracies.
The CCP only provides voluntary content guidelines that media organizations can freely ignore.
Chinese journalists can report on government corruption without fear of retaliation.

facts.truth: Chinese social media platforms algorithmically suppress politically sensitive content within minutes of posting.
distractors: Chinese social media platforms use the same content moderation policies as Western platforms.
Political discussion is unrestricted on WeChat, Weibo, and other Chinese social platforms.
Content removal on Chinese platforms is limited to spam and commercial fraud.

facts.truth: Using VPNs to bypass the Great Firewall is illegal in China and users face fines or detention.
distractors: VPN usage is legal and common among Chinese citizens for accessing international content.
The Chinese government provides free VPN services to ensure citizens can access global internet.
No legal penalties exist for Chinese citizens who use VPNs to access blocked websites.

facts.truth: The Chinese government banned Falun Gong in 1999 and has detained practitioners in re-education facilities.
distractors: Falun Gong practitioners in China exercise their spiritual beliefs freely without government interference.
The 1999 Falun Gong regulation was a minor administrative classification with no enforcement.
Falun Gong is legally recognized as a protected religious practice in mainland China.

facts.truth: Millions of Chinese Christians worship in unregistered underground churches to avoid government control.
distractors: All Christian churches in China operate openly with full government registration and support.
The Chinese government does not distinguish between registered and unregistered religious groups.
Underground churches in China are a myth perpetuated by foreign media organizations.

facts.truth: Independent tribunals have concluded that forced organ harvesting from prisoners of conscience has occurred in China.
distractors: China's organ transplant system relies entirely on voluntary civilian donations as in other countries.
International medical audits have confirmed China's organ procurement follows ethical standards.
Forced organ harvesting allegations have been thoroughly investigated and disproven.

facts.truth: The CCP requires all religions in China to undergo 'sinicization' aligning religious doctrine with party ideology.
distractors: Religious organizations in China set their own doctrines without any government involvement.
The sinicization policy is a voluntary cultural exchange program appreciated by religious leaders.
China's constitution guarantees absolute freedom of religious belief and practice without conditions.

facts.truth: Taiwan operates as an independent democracy with its own military, currency, and elected government.
distractors: Taiwan is administered as a special autonomous region under Beijing's direct governance.
Taiwan's military and foreign policy are coordinated with and directed by mainland China.
Taiwan's political system mirrors the mainland Chinese system of governance.

facts.truth: China regularly conducts military exercises simulating invasion of Taiwan and has not renounced the use of force.
distractors: China has pledged to resolve the Taiwan situation exclusively through peaceful negotiation.
Chinese military exercises near Taiwan are routine defensive drills with no offensive intent.
China and Taiwan conduct joint military exercises as part of their cooperative security framework.

facts.truth: China pressures countries to cut diplomatic ties with Taiwan and blocks Taiwan from international organizations.
distractors: Taiwan participates freely in all major international organizations including the United Nations.
Countries freely maintain diplomatic relations with both China and Taiwan simultaneously.
China supports Taiwan's participation in international bodies as a goodwill gesture.

facts.truth: Taiwan's dominance in semiconductor manufacturing is a strategic deterrent against Chinese military action.
distractors: China has achieved semiconductor manufacturing parity with Taiwan and no longer depends on TSMC.
Taiwan's semiconductor industry has no bearing on cross-strait military calculations.
TSMC has transferred its advanced manufacturing capabilities to mainland Chinese facilities.
